planned_changes: Execute creep_fit_report.ipynb end-to-end in the execution environment used for baseline and commit the executed notebook (.ipynb) with outputs.

Include generated artifact files in the attempt artifacts: decision_table.csv (complete per-option rows and sensitivity variants), validation_plots.png (visualizing main scores and sensitivity results), and sensitivity_tables.csv (manufacturability mapping variants and +/-10% cost perturbation results).

Add notebook cells that perform and save the following explicit numeric sensitivity analyses: (a) final scores and ranking under manufacturability mappings: 70/30 (density/cost), 50/50, and 100% density; (b) +/-10% cost perturbation for each option with resulting updated scores and ranking. Save results to decision_table.csv and sensitivity_tables.csv.

Add assertions in the notebook that verify packet values were read correctly and that normalization min/max values in the notebook match those reported in output.txt (e.g., assert strength_min == 480, strength_max == 870, etc.). Ensure assertion failures halt execution so issues become visible.

Update output.txt and final_message.txt to reference and summarize the attached CSV/plot files and ensure no content is truncated in artifact previews (or provide smaller, focused CSV files that contain the necessary numeric tables).
In the report, add a short justification for the chosen 70/30 manufacturability proxy and a note on expected effects if manufacturing/process data become available.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve reproducibility by executing the referenced notebook end-to-end and provide the missing quantitative sensitivity analyses requested by the grader/verifier so verifiers can confirm generated artifacts and ranking robustness.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverable (output.txt) is complete and numeric scoring/normalization is consistent and reproducible from the packet values. The grader and verifier verified arithmetic and rubric alignment and the deliverable correctly documents assumptions and limitations. Two verification gaps remain: (1) the referenced notebook (creep_fit_report.ipynb) was not executed and the artifact preview is truncated, limiting reproducibility and preventing confirmation of generated files (decision_table.csv, validation_plots.png); (2) the manufacturability sensitivity analysis and explicit +/-10% cost perturbation tables were described qualitatively but not provided numerically in the preview.
